semanticCore: Concealment, behind, striking fire, or fatness
quranicRelevance: Hiding/concealing (Muwaraat - 'yuwari'), behind/beyond (Wara' - 'min wara'ihi'), and striking fire (Wary - 'fal-mooriyati qadhan').

keyDerivations.meaning: To hide, conceal, cover, or shroud

figurativeOrRareUsages: يُوَارِي سَوْآةَ أَخِيهِ (5:31): To hide/shroud the corpse of his brother.
فَالمُورِيَاتِ قَدْحًا (100:2): And those that strike fire (sparks of hooves).
summaryEn: The root denotes concealment and friction, representing spatial behind (Wara'), the burial/concealing of dead bodies, and striking sparks.
summaryAr: يدل هذا الجذر على المواراة والاحتكاك، ممثلاً الجهة الخلفية (وراء)، ومواراة الموتى، وقدح الشرر بالحوافر.
rawText: أَوْرَى 4 and 🡻وَرَّى and 🡻اِسْتَوْرَى He made his زند to produce fire. (S, K.) === See 1. إِسْتَوْرَىَ 10 see 4. النَّوْرَاةُ The Book of the Law revealed to Moses. (Bd, iii. 2; &c.) رِيَةٌ : see رِئَةٌ, in art. رأى. وَارَاهُ 3 He hid it, concealed it, or covered it. (S, Msb, K, &c.) وَرَائِى كَذَا Behind me is such a thing, as though it were a burden upon my back. === مِنْ وَرَآءَ وَرَآءَ From behind a thing covering, or concealing. (TA.) === فُلَانٌ مِنْ وَرَآءِ فُلَانٍ Such a one is an aider of such a one: or a follower. (Ham, p. 206.) === اَللّٰهُ مِنْ وَرَائِكَ God is seeking after thee, and watching, or lying in wait, for thee. (Ham, p. 206.) See also an ex. in the first paragraph of art. فتل. وَرَى 1 , aor. يَرِى, inf. n. وَرْىٌ [and وُرِىٌّ and رِيَةٌ, K]; and وَرِىَ, aor. يَرِى; and 🡻أَوْرَى; It (a زَنْد) produced its fire. (Msb.) === وَرِيَتْ بِكَ زِنَادِى, or وَرَتْ: see art. زند, and see وَقَدَ and زَهَرَ, and راى. === قَدْحٌ لَا يُورِى: see خَيَّابٌ. وَرَّى بِشَىْءٍ عَنْ شَىْءٍ 2 [He pretended, or made believe, a thing, instead of a thing which he meant: as is shown by the explanation of a trad. in the TA]. (S, art. عرض; save that the inf. n. is there mentioned instead of the pret.) === وَرَّى عَنْ كَذَا, inf. n. تَوْرِيَةٌ, He alluded to such a thing equivocally, or ambiguously: equivocated respecting it: he meant such a thing and pretended another. (M, K.) التورية is also called الإِيهَامُ and التَّوْجِيهُ and التَّخْيِيلُ: (Kull, p. 113:) and signifies The using a word, an expression, or a phrase, which has an obvious meaning, and intending thereby another meaning, to which it applies, but which is contrary to the obvious one. (Msb.) See مِعْرَاضٌ. === See 4. وَرْىٌ Purulent matter in the interior of the body: or [an abscess; or] a severe ulcer that discharges purulent matter and blood. (M, K, TA.) وَرْيًا وَقُحَابًا: see قُحَابٌ.
